name: Haili's Hawaiian Food
tagline: Family-recipe Hawaiian food since 1950 — laulau, kalua pig, and squid luau that rival Helena's without the hype.
mustOrder: Laulau — tender pork wrapped in taro and ti leaves, steamed for hours
Kalua pig plate — smoky, salty, fall-apart pulled pork
Squid luau — squid simmered in creamy taro leaf
parking: Small lot in front on North King St. Street parking available
bestTime: Lunch rush (11am–1pm) can have lines. Go early or after 2pm
talkStory: Operating since 1950, Haili's is the less-hyped sibling to Helena's Hawaiian Food — but locals will argue it's just as good. The recipes come from Aunty Haili's family and haven't changed in decades. The laulau is excellent — tender pork with butterfish wrapped in taro leaves — and the kalua pig is properly smoky. It's a no-frills plate lunch spot in a part of town most tourists never visit. If Helena's line is too long or they're closed (they're only open limited days), Haili's is the backup the locals know about.
